title: litsea elongata var. suberosa
intro: Litsea elongata var. suberosa, also known as the Prickly Bay tree, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its prickly bark and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be utilized to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and skin infections. The health benefits of Litsea elongata var. suberosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is also used in poultices and salves to treat skin conditions.
